@graph.schema:name: DK-D01.2-02 Mobile species - abundance - seals
@graph.schema:description: Spotted seals have been monitored since 1979 and gray seals since 2011. It is assumed that the extent of the current NOVANA 2017-21 program will essentially be maintained. This partly concerns activities targeted at the Marine Strategy Directive, but also activities that are originally based on needs related to the Habitats Directive. Gray seal program changes: Under the current program, areas in the Kattegat are not monitored during the breeding and trapping periods of the North Sea population, from which most gray seals in the Kattegat originate. Therefore, the areas in the Kattegat will in future be monitored by two counts during the population's breeding period and one during the trapping period. Program changes for harbor seals: In 2021, a pilot project for mapping harbor seals in the South Funen Archipelago and southern Little Belt will be carried out. The addition of this area is due to the fact that historically there have been more resting places in these areas, and that in recent years there have been reports of larger occurrences here.

schema:geo.schema:description: Bounding box polygon with lat long (Y X) coordinate order.
